agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Critical SQL injection vulnerability — user-controlled inputs are interpolated directly into raw SQL with no parameterization or sanitization."
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "SQL Injection via unparameterized f-string query",
      "risk": "Full database compromise. An attacker can read, modify, or delete any data, bypass authentication, or execute administrative operations depending on DB permissions.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — query construction on lines 2-6",
      "reproduction": "Call the public API with: status_filter=active' OR '1'='1' -- to dump all deliveries for all customers. Or: customer_id=' UNION SELECT username, password, null, null FROM users -- to exfiltrate credentials. Or: status_filter='; DROP TABLE deliveries; -- for destructive payloads.",
      "fix": "Use parameterized queries: query = 'SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?'; result = db_conn.execute(query, (customer_id, status_filter)). Use ? or %s placeholders depending on your DB driver (sqlite3 uses ?, psycopg2 uses %s). Never interpolate user input into SQL strings.",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security — OWASP A03:2021 Injection"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on public API parameters",
      "risk": "Even after fixing the injection, missing validation means unbounded or malformed inputs hit the database layer. Empty customer_id returns unexpected results; extremely long strings could degrade performance.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — function signature / entry point",
      "reproduction": "Call with customer_id='' — returns all deliveries where customer_id is empty string (likely unintended). Call with a 10MB string — no length guard.",
      "fix": "Validate customer_id is non-empty and matches expected format (e.g., UUID regex). Validate status_filter against an allowlist of known statuses (e.g., {'pending', 'in_transit', 'delivered', 'cancelled'}). Reject invalid inputs before reaching the DB.",
      "time_to_fix": "10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Input validation, defense in depth"
    },
    {
      "title": "SELECT * exposes internal columns to public API consumers",
      "risk": "SELECT * returns every column including potentially sensitive fields (internal notes, driver PII, cost data, soft-delete flags). Schema changes silently alter the API response, risking data leaks and breaking clients.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — SQL query",
      "reproduction": "Add a column like internal_notes or driver_ssn to the deliveries table — it is immediately returned to all public API callers with no code change required.",
      "fix": "Enumerate columns explicitly: SELECT id, customer_id, status, estimated_delivery, tracking_number FROM deliveries ... Only select fields intended for the public response.",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data privacy, API stability"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No pagination or result limit",
      "description": "A customer with thousands of deliveries returns all rows in one call. This is a denial-of-service vector and a memory/latency risk under production load. Add LIMIT/OFFSET or cursor-based pagination.",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — SQL query"
    },
    {
      "title": "No authorization check — any customer_id accepted",
      "description": "The function trusts the caller-supplied customer_id. A user can query another customer's deliveries by changing the parameter. The calling layer must enforce that the authenticated user owns the requested customer_id.",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — function entry"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Wrap the DB call in a try/except to handle connection errors and return a clean error response instead of a 500.",
    "Add an index on (customer_id, status) if one doesn't exist — this query will be slow at scale without it.",
    "Return typed dataclass/Pydantic objects instead of raw tuples from fetchall() for safer serialization."
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "This code is actively exploitable the moment it reaches any public-facing environment. The SQL injection alone is a P0 stop-ship.",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — this should not be deployed in its current form.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Switching to parameterized queries is backward-compatible for all legitimate callers. Only malicious payloads change behavior.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging of queries or failed attempts. After fixing injection, add request logging to detect probing attempts.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": ["SQL injection allows unauthorized read/write/delete of any database data accessible by the connection's DB user"]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Do not deploy. Fix the injection, add validation, then re-review."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 99,
    "high_confidence_areas": ["SQL injection is unambiguous and trivially exploitable", "Input validation gap is clear from the function signature", "SELECT * risk is a well-known anti-pattern for public APIs"],
    "low_confidence_areas": ["Cannot confirm authorization enforcement without seeing the calling API layer", "Cannot confirm DB user permissions (which bounds the blast radius of injection)"],
    "assumptions": ["db_conn.execute supports parameterized queries (true for all standard Python DB-API 2.0 drivers)", "This function is reachable from the public internet as stated"]
  }
}

content.thinking: SQL injection vulnerability, plain and simple.

      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security — OWASP A03:2021 Injection"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on public API parameters",
      "risk": "Even after fixing the injection, missing validation means unbounded or malformed inputs hit the database layer. Empty customer_id returns unexpected results; extremely long strings could degrade performance.",

      "time_to_fix": "10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Input validation, defense in depth"
    },
    {
      "title": "SELECT * exposes internal columns to public API consumers",
      "risk": "SELECT * returns every column including potentially sensitive fields (internal notes, driver PII, cost data, soft-delete flags). Schema changes silently alter the API response, risking data leaks and breaking clients.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — SQL query",
      "reproduction": "Add a column like internal_notes or driver_ssn to the deliveries table — it is immediately returned to all public API callers with no code change required.",
      "fix": "Enumerate columns explicitly: SELECT id, customer_id, status, estimated_delivery, tracking_number FROM deliveries ... Only select fields intended for the public response.",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data privacy, API stability"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No pagination or result limit",
      "description": "A customer with thousands of deliveries returns all rows in one call. This is a denial-of-service vector and a memory/latency risk under production load. Add LIMIT/OFFSET or cursor-based pagination.",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — SQL query"
    },
    {
      "title": "No authorization check — any customer_id accepted",
      "description": "The function trusts the caller-supplied customer_id. A user can query another customer's deliveries by changing the parameter. The calling layer must enforce that the authenticated user owns the requested customer_id.",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — function entry"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Wrap the DB call in a try/except to handle connection errors and return a clean error response instead of a 500.",
    "Add an index on (customer_id, status) if one doesn't exist — this query will be slow at scale without it.",
    "Return typed dataclass/Pydantic objects instead of raw tuples from fetchall() for safer serialization."
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "This code is actively exploitable the moment it reaches any public-facing environment. The SQL injection alone is a P0 stop-ship.",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — this should not be deployed in its current form.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Switching to parameterized queries is backward-compatible for all legitimate callers. Only malicious payloads change behavior.",

      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": ["SQL injection allows unauthorized read/write/delete of any database data accessible by the connection's DB user"]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Do not deploy. Fix the injection, add validation, then re-review."
  },

  "escalations": [],
